stories.headline: Seeing 'The Odyssey' in 70mm IMAX in Michigan is not easy - Detroit Free Press
stories.summary: Only two Michigan theaters are showing Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in the rare 70mm IMAX format.
stories.why: Nolan's 70mm IMAX is the theatrical experience that justifies leaving your couch—scarcity makes it culturally significant for cinephiles who still believe movies demand to be seen big.

stories.headline: Judge says Paramount and Warner must halt merger for at least two weeks, granting states' request - AP News
stories.summary: A federal judge has ordered Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery to halt their $81 billion merger for at least two weeks, allowing states that are challenging the deal more time to see their case thr…
stories.why: This isn't just corporate news; it signals whether streaming consolidation will throttle your viewing options and original content, affecting what you can actually watch in 5 years.

stories.headline: ‘Scrubs’ Promotes Ava Bunn to Series Regular for Season 2, Joel Kim Booster and X Mayo Depart - The Hollywood Reporter
stories.summary: Stars Zach Braff, Sarah Chalke and Donald Faison are also set to return as regulars.
stories.why: The reboot's casting shifts show which Gen X nostalgia plays are still bankable and who gets second chances in prestige TV—relevant if you're deciding whether to actually watch the revival.

stories.headline: 'Today' suspect caught on camera before hate crime arrest, rap sheet reveals attempted kidnapping conviction - Fox News
stories.summary: Andrew Truelove's criminal record spans two decades, including attempted kidnapping and making threats, before his alleged hate crime involving Craig Melvin.
stories.why: A violent crime allegedly caught on camera by a 'Today' producer undercuts the morning show's trusted-authority brand in a #MeToo moment that exposes institutional blind spots.

stories.headline: Former Miss Universe Jamaica finalist LaToya Malcolm dead at 35 - Page Six
stories.summary: The organization announced the tragic news via Instagram over the weekend.
stories.why: Another young woman dead at 35 in an industry that peddles impossible beauty standards—her death matters as a pattern, not a headline, if you have daughters or care about systemic harm.

stories.headline: Sylvia Bugg, PBS Programming Chief, Dies at 55 - The Hollywood Reporter
stories.summary: PBS CEO Paula Kerger announced the news on Sunday.
stories.why: PBS's programming chief dying at 55 signals vulnerability in the one institution that's been reliably serving serious culture; her loss is felt most by men who actually watch documentaries.

stories.headline: Robbie Williams responds to claims cocaine fell from his face onto microphone during World Cup TV interview - NME
stories.summary: Robbie Williams has responded to claims that a white object falling onto his microphone during a World Cup television interview was cocaine.
stories.why: Whether it's cocaine or not, Robbie Williams's image took damage in a live moment—this is about how one viral clip can reframe a legacy faster than decades of work.

stories.headline: Prince George Reportedly Asked His Parents if He Could Take This Major Step That “Helped Him Grow” - instyle.com
stories.summary: Prince George asked his parents, Prince William and Kate Middleton, if he could begin boarding at Lambrook to prepare him for life at secondary school, according to royal biographer Robert Jobson. Ge…
stories.why: A royal kid choosing boarding school for growth signals a generational shift in parenting even at the highest privilege level, reflecting broader debates about independence vs. protection that hit home for fathers.
